goal: Inspect /app/weights.pt and /app/dataset.pt, derive the RecoveredModel architecture, load weights strictly, and compute the baseline MSE loss on the dataset.
summary: Phase 1 complete: architecture recovered (transformer encoder-decoder with Linear(64→128) projection + sinusoidal PE + 3-layer encoder + 1-layer decoder + Linear(128→64) output, d_model=128, nhead=8, dim_ff=256, batch_first=True). RecoveredModel in /app/model.py strictly loads /app/weights.pt. Baseline MSE = 1.5510306358337402 on /app/dataset.pt. OUTPUT_LAYER_KEY identified as literal 'output_layer'.
stepSummaries:
1. Run a small inspection script that loads /app/weights.pt (with map_location='cpu' and weights_only=True if available) and prints every key with shape and dtype; also loads /app/dataset.pt and prints the structure (type, shapes, dtypes) so the dataset's (X, y) pair is identified. Write the inspection summary to /app/.kimchi/docs/inspect.md. [verified]
  2. Implement RecoveredModel in /app/model.py. Use the inspected shapes/dtypes to define nn.Linear / nn.Conv / activations / BatchNorm etc. so that model.load_state_dict(state_dict, strict=True) succeeds. Include a small self-test at the bottom that asserts strict load and prints a baseline forward pass on a batch from /app/dataset.pt. [verified]
  3. Compute baseline MSE: load /app/weights.pt into RecoveredModel in eval() mode, run forward over /app/dataset.pt, compute MSE between predictions and targets. Save baseline MSE value and the target key naming decision to /app/.kimchi/docs/baseline.md. [verified]
reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Step 1 (inspect.md) was smoke — direct torch.load execution. Step 2 (model.py) used the prescribed verify command end-to-end (real test: load_state_dict strict). Step 3 (baseline.md) used smoke — model.py self-test output. Mixed but the load-bearing strict-load verification is real.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Step 2 verify command: `cd /app && python -c "import torch; from model import RecoveredModel; sd = torch.load('/app/weights.pt', map_location='cpu'); m = RecoveredModel(); m.load_state_dict(sd, strict=True); print('strict load OK')"` exited 0 with 'strict load OK'.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Phase goal: derive RecoveredModel architecture, load weights strictly, compute baseline MSE. All three are delivered — /app/model.py exists and strict-loads, baseline MSE 1.5510 saved to baseline.md.
Nothing deferred — all three planned steps completed. nhead correction (3→8) was a fix within step 2, not a deferral.
